meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome debugging hurdles. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your coding workflow.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By pivoting to an AI-first design workflow, you treat code as a modular system rather than a monolith. Using Antigravity to architect logic and Claude Code to execute implementation, you bypass manual debugging by generating self-correcting, validated code structures from the start.
content.intro:
You’re three hours deep into a project, staring at a wall of red text that makes no sense. The frustration is visceral—you’re a student trying to build, but you feel like you’re fighting the compiler more than writing the software. We’ve all been there, stuck in the 'debug loop' where fixing one line breaks three others.

But the industry is changing. Top-tier developers no longer 'fix' bugs; they design them out of existence. By leveraging AI-driven architectural tools, you shift the burden from manual troubleshooting to high-level design. This case study will transform how you approach your codebase, turning your biggest struggle into your competitive advantage.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Constraint
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, use Antigravity to map out the state flow. This prevents logical bugs before they are ever implemented.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this feature requirement [Insert Requirements] and generate a state machine diagram that highlights potential edge cases and race conditions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude init --context 'Generate the core state machine schema based on the Antigravity diagram'

steps.title: Bootstrap with AI-Validated Boilerplate
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the skeletal structure of your modules, ensuring strict type safety and interface definitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust directory structure and boilerplate code that adheres to the state machine defined in the previous step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate modular boilerplate for [Feature Name] focusing on dependency injection'

steps.title: Implement Defensive Logic Blocks
steps.description: Instead of writing raw logic, ask the AI to wrap your functional requirements in comprehensive error-handling layers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a set of validation schemas for my API endpoints that prevent invalid data from propagating through the state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement input sanitization and Zod schemas for all exported functions'

steps.title: Automated Unit Test Synthesis
steps.description: Before running the app, generate tests that simulate failure scenarios to ensure your logic is truly bulletproof.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite covering the happy path and three critical failure modes for my current implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --generate 'Create unit tests for all edge cases identified in the Antigravity analysis'

steps.title: Refactor for Observability
steps.description: Add logging and performance monitoring hooks so that if a bug does appear, it identifies itself immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest where to place telemetry hooks in my codebase to monitor state transitions effectively.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'Insert structured logging at every state transition point without changing business logic'

pro_tips: Treat your AI assistant as a junior pair programmer, not a magic wand; always review the generated imports.
Use Antigravity to visualize dependencies before adding new libraries to prevent 'dependency hell'.
If Claude Code suggests a complex solution, ask it to 'simplify to the most readable pattern' to maintain maintainability.
Always keep a 'scratchpad' file for AI to reference so it maintains context across long sessions.
When a bug persists, ask the AI to 'explain the bug in terms of data flow' rather than asking it to fix the code directly.
content.next_steps: Apply these steps to your existing project, document your reduction in 'debugging time', and prepare to show this workflow in your next technical interview.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build faster and debug less. Stop fighting your code and start architecting your success. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
